description: Topological data analysis of integer representations (persistent homology via ripser, Mapper, circular coordinates) to fit a periodic helix, then a causal 'Clock' intervention: rotate the helix component by 2*pi/T (T=10) and decode whether the answer shifts. Driver: modal_app.py causal_clock_suite.
motivation: Ask whether the circular integer code is causally consumed at answer-commit.
formal_results: Integers lie on a helix (H1 period ~10, sinusoidal variance explained 0.20-0.41; FT sharpens it, e.g. 7B L20 R2 0.378 -> 0.425). But at the answer-commit position, no_change = 1.00 under T=10 rotation for every model - the circle is not consumed there. At the L0 input position no_change drops to 0.43-0.70 but shifts are mostly absolute, not clock-modular.
results_description: Integers form a circular code, but rotating it does not change the committed answer - the Clock is not causally used at answer-commit.
conclusions: Inconclusive and DELIBERATELY PULLED from the paper: the helix exists but the causal Clock result lacks the controls (random-direction, magnitude-sweep) to publish.

comments.pulled: Pulled from the paper 2026-05-29 (HELIX_NOTES.md): the no_change=1.00 result lacks random-direction / magnitude / positive-direction controls, so it is not safe to publish as a negative claim.
